query: useMemo vs useCallback in React: what is the difference?

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this React prompt, so broader React question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: useCallback
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical React reference because it explicitly explains how useCallback relates to useMemo and when caching function identity matters.
theirStrengths: It is the authoritative React explanation of cached function identity, child rerender avoidance, and the relation between useCallback and useMemo.
theirGaps: It is centered on useCallback itself, while our page answers the exact compare-and-choose interview question more directly.

realWorldUseCases: useMemo() and useCallback() help improve performance by caching results or function references between re-renders.
Overusing them can make code complex without significant performance gain.
followUpConfusion: useMemo() recalculates a value only when dependencies change.
useCallback() returns the same function reference between renders unless its dependencies change.

realWorldUseCases: The docs are authoritative, but our page is more compressed around the exact real-world choice: expensive value versus stable callback prop.
